findings.title: Simulation mode can no longer dwell-click in the browser: event-driven forwarding starves the page dwell loop and the gap pause cancels the catch-up commit
findings.scenario: Run `.\start-dev.bat --simulate` (or have the Tobii drop out mid-session â€” RealGazeContext.tsx:31,50 auto-flips hasRealGaze=false after 1.5s of no frames, switching the app to mouse-as-gaze). Open YouTube/news in the embedded browser, move the mouse onto a link, and hold it still â€” which is exactly how a person dwells with a mouse. The in-page cursor freezes mid-dwell: the ring never progresses and the click never fires, no matter how long they hover. Nudging the mouse afterwards also does nothing. The embedded browser is unclickable in simulation/fallback mode; on the 9183f5d baseline this worked.
findings.evidence: Before this range, forwarding was an unconditional 33ms setInterval (9183f5d WebBrowsingScreen.tsx:3749-3797) that re-sent gpRef every tick, so a stationary mouse still produced 30Hz calls to gcUpdateAndPoll and the wall-clock dwell (`elapsed = now - state.start`) committed after dwellMs. Commit 52cb308 made forwarding event-driven: `ws.subscribeGaze(() => forward())` (never fires in simulation â€” no WS gaze frames) plus `onMouse = () => { if (!hasRealGazeRef.current) forward(); }` (WebBrowsingScreen.tsx:3854-3860). A stationary mouse emits no mousemove events, so gcUpdateAndPoll is never invoked and the page dwell cannot tick â€” the commit check at browserGazeController.ts:1491 (`elapsed > dwellMs && !state.clicked && clickReq`) only runs per invocation. Pre-d9fb816 the first nudge after >1.1s of stillness would still commit (elapsed had accumulated in wall time); commit d9fb816's wrapper now sees `dtMs > gapPauseMs(150)` on that nudge and executes `state.start += dtMs` (browserGazeController.ts:1611), erasing the accumulated time. To click, the user would have to keep the mouse continuously moving (events <150ms apart) while staying inside the ~60-130px stability radius for the full onset+dwell 1380ms â€” natural hover-and-hold accumulates zero progress. This directly breaks the stated invariant that simulation mode must still drive the in-page browser cursor, and contradicts docs/EYE_TRACKING_CHANGES.md Entry 9 ("a mousemove path keeps simulation mode working").

findings.title: Gap shift pushes state.start into the future when a wall-clock writer re-armed it mid-gap (navigation reset / post-click timer), silently freezing dwell for up to ~1-2s
findings.scenario: Patient dwell-clicks a YouTube card at Tc and blinks or closes their eyes through the page change (gaze frames stop). 900ms into the blink, the post-click setTimeout fires and sets state.start = Date.now() (line 1560); alternatively did-navigate-in-page / did-start-navigation fires mid-gap and gcResetDwell sets state.start = Date.now() (line 1090) â€” on YouTube these SPA/iframe navigation events fire routinely 0.3-2s after a click. When the eyes reopen 2s later, the first frame's dtMs covers the whole gap, and the wrapper adds it to a start timestamp that was written DURING the gap: state.start lands up to ~0.9s (timer) or ~2s (slow navigation) in the FUTURE of the wall clock. Because gcResetDwell and the timer leave state.x/y at the pre-gap card anchor, the patient resuming gaze on the same screen spot passes stabilityHeld (dist < 130px card radius, line 1274/1288), so the not-held branch that would re-seed start = now (line 1338) is skipped and the future start survives. elapsed = now - state.start is negative: no onset ring (line 1441), no commit (line 1491) â€” the dwell is silently dead with zero visual feedback until the wall clock catches up, then the full onset+dwell starts on top. Patient experience: after clicking a video and blinking, staring at the next target does nothing for up to ~1-2 extra seconds â€” time-to-click roughly doubles, unpredictably, in the primary input loop of a medical AAC device.
findings.evidence: The shift `if (state.start > 0) state.start += dtMs;` (line 1611) assumes state.start predates the gap, but three writers set it to Date.now() BETWEEN frames: gcResetDwell (lines 1089-1110, wired to did-start-navigation/did-navigate/did-navigate-in-page at main.ts:1355/1359/1364), gcBlockDwell (line 1119), and the post-click cooldown setTimeout (line 1560). For a writer firing at T_write inside a gap [T_gapStart, T_resume], the resume frame computes dtMs â‰ˆ T_resume - T_gapStart and produces state.start = T_write + dtMs = T_resume + (T_write - T_gapStart) â€” strictly in the future. None of these writers clear state.x/state.y (only the not-held branch at lines 1331-1332 updates them), so same-spot gaze resume keeps stabilityHeld true and the corrupted start is never corrected. Downstream: dwellState telemetry in the wrapper (lines 1623-1632) computes el2 = nowMs - state.start < 0 and falls through to label the frame 'onset' while the dwell is actually inert, corrupting the frames ring used for on-rig tuning. The commit message claims progress "freezes, never advances or resets" â€” this path adds a hidden penalty instead. Fix shape: clamp the shift, e.g. `state.start = Math.min(state.start + dtMs, Date.now())` (same for savedProgressAt), or skip the shift when state.start already exceeds Date.now() - dtMs.
